success_surface: Cell-level formula insertion with text parsing and data validation
effective_behaviors: Comprehensive initial data exploration using multiple library methods before solution coding
Excel formula construction instead of in-memory Python calculation for cell-level tasks
Robust formula design with error handling for edge cases (blank cells, missing keywords)
Proper file handling pattern: read source, write to new output path with directory creation
Post-execution verification by reloading the output file and checking cell values/formulas
Raw string formula writing preserving Excel's formula syntax (= prefix, no evaluation)
evidence: Agent used both pandas.read_excel() and openpyxl.load_workbook() to inspect sheet names, dimensions, and column content before coding
Created nested IF formula with ISBLANK, SEARCH, FIND, MID, LEFT functions to handle text parsing and null safety
Saved to output_path instead of modifying init.xlsx; created output_dir with os.makedirs(exist_ok=True)
Reopened saved file with load_workbook() to verify B2 contained correct formula value before confirming success
Used '=IF(...)' format ensuring Excel interprets as formula not static text
Checked max_row and max_column programmatically to confirm data extent before applying changes

memory_items.title: Pre-Coding Data Inspection Protocol
memory_items.lesson: Before implementing solutions, exhaustively explore spreadsheet structure using both high-level (pandas) and low-level (openpyxl) tools
memory_items.generalization: For any spreadsheet task, first programmatically determine sheet names, active sheet, row/column extents, and sample cell values across target columns to inform strategy selection

memory_items.title: Cell-Level Formula vs Python Calculation Decision
memory_items.lesson: For cell-level manipulations where dynamic recalculation is needed, prefer Excel formulas over static Python-calculated values
memory_items.generalization: When answer_position specifies individual cells or small ranges requiring dependency on other cell values, construct Excel formulas written as raw strings starting with '=' rather than precomputing in Python

memory_items.title: Text Parsing Formula Pattern
memory_items.lesson: Complex text parsing within Excel should use nested IF with ISBLANK for null safety and SEARCH/FIND for delimiter detection
memory_items.generalization: When processing text-formatted data within Excel formulas, always include defensive checks for empty cells and missing expected delimiters to prevent formula errors

memory_items.title: Verification Before Confirmation
memory_items.lesson: Always reopen the saved output file to verify that formulas were written correctly and not converted to static values
memory_items.generalization: After saving modified workbooks, perform a second load operation to confirm cell values match expected formulas, catching any serialization issues before declaring success

memory_items.title: Output Path Safety Pattern
memory_items.lesson: Create output directory if missing and never overwrite source files during execution
memory_items.generalization: Always use os.makedirs(output_dir, exist_ok=True) before wb.save(), and treat input paths as read-only regardless of task requirements

edits.content:
## 6. Text-Parsing Formulas & Validation
- **Formula Selection**: For cell-level tasks requiring dynamic updates based on other cell values, write Excel formulas instead of pre-computed Python values.
- **Parsing Pattern**: When extracting data from text strings in formulas, combine ISBLANK, SEARCH, FIND, MID, and LEFT/RIGHT functions with nested IF conditions for robustness.
- **Double-Check Writes**: After saving, reload the output workbook programmatically and verify that formula cells retain their formula strings (not evaluated values).
- **Directory Safety**: Ensure output directories exist via os.makedirs(os.path.dirname(path), exist_ok=True) before saving.
